reason: Both answers correctly compute LTV ($3,200), LTV:CAC (4:1), and CAC payback (~8.3 months), and correctly flag 3% monthly churn as the weak point investors will probe, with mention of NRR as a mitigating factor. Answer B is tighter and adds a concrete detail (implied avg lifetime ~33 months) without missteps. Answer A has useful extras but pads more and closes with a vague pivot claiming growth rate matters more than unit economics, muddying its bottom line. B delivers the same substance more cleanly.
